commands.summary: Backend TypeScript build passed with schema v10 call rows, Twilio Voice adapter support, and call API/webhook routes.

commands.command: node --test backend-dist/channels.test.js backend-dist/twilio.test.js backend-dist/database.test.js backend-dist/migration.test.js backend-dist/server.test.js
commands.summary: Focused backend coverage passed: 40/40 tests. Coverage includes same-provider SMS/Voice registry selection, Twilio Voice start/end and parsers, durable call rows, idempotent status callbacks, inbound call-row creation, invalid signature rejection, and redacted provider errors.
Full Electron suite green: 77 tests passed and 1 opt-in live Twilio test skipped by design. Backend build, renderer build, renderer tests, backend node tests, and runtime syntax checks passed.
Local system audit passed after marking CLV-013 satisfied: required files, 18 work items, lifecycle counts, stale README checks, work item manifests, and Markdown link/date checks are current.

commands.summary: RepoPact records are current: dashboard regenerated, doctor reports healthy, and repository governance validation passed.

commands.command: npm run scan:secrets && npm run scan:deps
commands.summary: Secret scan clean and production dependency audit reports 0 vulnerabilities.

environment.process_note: 015 CLV-013 (Twilio Voice edge). Twilio Voice is implemented as a provider-neutral voice_edge adapter with outbound call start/end, signed Twilio voice status callbacks, inbound call-row creation, schema v10 durable calls, idempotent lifecycle transitions, and redacted provider errors. The TwiML endpoint intentionally provides minimal call-control plumbing only; media bridge, recording, transcription, voicemail, emergency calling, CNAM, STIR/SHAKEN, and E911 behavior remain out of scope until separate criteria exist.
